L2().details: Sets up the L2 contracts. Depends on `L1()` being called first.

disableUpgradedFork().details: Disables upgrade mode for testing. By default the fork testing env will be upgraded to the latest      implementation. This can be used to disable the upgrade which, is useful for tests targeting the upgrade      process itself.

enableAltDA().details: Enables alternative data availability mode for testing

returnIfForkTest(string).details: Returns early when running against a forked production network. Useful for allowing a portion of a test      to run.

skipIfCoverage().details: Skips tests when running in coverage mode.

skipIfForkTest(string).details: Skips tests when running against a forked production network.

skipIfOpsRepoTest(string).details: Skips tests when running against a forked production network using the superchain ops repo.

test_bridgeERC721To_localTokenZeroAddres.details: Tests that `bridgeERC721To` reverts if the local token is the zero address.

test_bridgeERC721To_remoteTokenZeroAddre.details: Tests that `bridgeERC721To` reverts if the remote token is the zero address.

test_bridgeERC721To_succeeds().details: Tests that `bridgeERC721To` correctly bridges a token      and burns it on the origin chain.

test_bridgeERC721To_toZeroAddress_revert.details: Tests that `bridgeERC721To` reverts if the to address is the zero address.

test_bridgeERC721To_wrongOwner_reverts().details: Tests that `bridgeERC721To` reverts if the caller is not the token owner.

test_bridgeERC721_fromContract_reverts().details: Tests that `bridgeERC721` reverts if the owner is not an EOA.

test_bridgeERC721_localTokenZeroAddress_.details: Tests that `bridgeERC721` reverts if the local token is the zero address.

test_bridgeERC721_remoteTokenZeroAddress.details: Tests that `bridgeERC721` reverts if the remote token is the zero address.

test_bridgeERC721_succeeds().details: Tests that `bridgeERC721` correctly bridges a token and      burns it on the origin chain.

test_bridgeERC721_wrongOwner_reverts().details: Tests that `bridgeERC721` reverts if the caller is not the token owner.

test_constructor_succeeds().details: Tests that the constructor sets the correct variables.

test_finalizeBridgeERC721_alreadyExists_.details: Tests that `finalizeBridgeERC721` reverts when already finalized.

test_finalizeBridgeERC721_interfaceNotCo.details: Tests that `finalizeBridgeERC721` reverts if the token is not compliant      with the `IOptimismMintableERC721` interface.

test_finalizeBridgeERC721_notFromRemoteM.details: Tests that `finalizeBridgeERC721` reverts when not called by the remote bridge.

test_finalizeBridgeERC721_notViaLocalMes.details: Tests that `finalizeBridgeERC721` reverts when not called by the remote bridge.

test_finalizeBridgeERC721_selfToken_reve.details: Tests that `finalizeBridgeERC721` reverts when the local token is the      address of the bridge itself.

test_finalizeBridgeERC721_succeeds().details: Tests that `finalizeBridgeERC721` correctly finalizes a bridged token.

test_paused_succeeds().details: Ensures that the L2ERC721Bridge is always not paused. The pausability      happens on L1 and not L2.

artifacts().notice: The address of the Artifacts contract. Set into state by Deployer.setUp() with `etch` to avoid         mutating any nonces. MUST not have constructor logic.

isForkTest().notice: Indicates whether a test is running against a forked production network.
